title: Quiz: Quantum Cloud API Access and Authentication

questions.question: What is the recommended priority order for loading IBM Quantum credentials?

options.text: Credentials file first, then environment variables
Environment variables first, then credentials file

questions.explanation: Environment variables should take priority over credentials files because they allow runtime configuration without modifying stored credentials, useful for CI/CD pipelines and containerized deployments.

questions.question: Which AWS service is used for authentication when accessing Amazon Braket?
questions.explanation: Amazon Braket uses AWS IAM for authentication. Users need IAM credentials (access key and secret key) or IAM roles to access Braket services and submit quantum tasks.

questions.question: What is the purpose of exponential backoff in API retry logic?

options.text: To gradually increase wait time between retries and avoid overwhelming the server

questions.explanation: Exponential backoff gradually increases the delay between retry attempts (e.g., 1s, 2s, 4s, 8s). This prevents overwhelming a struggling server and gives it time to recover from transient issues.

questions.question: What file permission mode should be used for credential files containing API tokens?
questions.explanation: Mode 0600 (rw-------) allows only the owner to read and write the file. This prevents other users on the system from reading sensitive credentials, following the principle of least privilege.

questions.question: How does Google Quantum AI primarily handle authentication?

options.text: Service account JSON key files or application default credentials

questions.explanation: Google Quantum AI uses Google Cloud Platform authentication, typically through service account JSON key files or application default credentials set up via 'gcloud auth application-default login'.

questions.question: What should NOT be done with quantum API credentials?

options.text: Commit them to version control repositories
Store them in encrypted credential managers

questions.explanation: API credentials should NEVER be committed to version control. Even in private repositories, credentials in git history can be exposed if the repo becomes public or is compromised.

questions.question: Rate limiting in quantum cloud APIs only affects how many circuits you can submit, not how many status queries you can make.
questions.explanation: False. Rate limiting typically applies to all API requests, including job submissions, status queries, backend listings, and other operations. Each request counts against your rate limit quota.

questions.question: Which error type should NOT trigger automatic retry logic?
questions.explanation: Authentication failures should not be automatically retried because they indicate invalid credentials. Retrying won't help and may lock out the account. Instead, the error should be raised immediately for the user to fix.
